an3.64:1.10: And having understood the *dhamma* of the ascetics, the sons of the Sakyan, that is why I have left that *dhamma* and *vinaya* [*dhammavinayā*]."
an3.64:1.2: at one time, the *Bhagavā* was dwelling at Rājagaha, on Vulture Peak mountain.
an3.64:1.3: Now at that time, a wanderer named Sarabha had recently left this *dhamma* and *vinaya* [*dhammavinayā*].
an3.64:1.4: He was speaking these words in an assembly at Rājagaha:
an3.64:1.5: "I have understood the *dhamma* of the ascetics, the sons of the Sakyan.
an3.64:1.6: And having understood the *dhamma* of the ascetics, the sons of the Sakyan, that is why I have left that *dhamma* and *vinaya* [*dhammavinayā*]."
an3.64:1.7: Then several *bhikkhus*, having dressed in the morning, took their bowls and robes and entered Rājagaha for alms.
an3.64:1.8: Those *bhikkhus* heard Sarabha the wanderer speaking these words in an assembly at Rājagaha:
an3.64:1.9: "I have understood the *dhamma* of the ascetics, the sons of the Sakyan.
an3.64:10.1: Whoever, wanderers, were to say this to me:
an3.64:10.2: 'The *dhamma* you have taught for a certain purpose does not lead one who practices it to the complete ending of *dukkha*' — that person I would thoroughly question, probe, and examine on this matter.
an3.64:10.3: That person, when thoroughly questioned, probed, and examined by me — it is impossible, there is no opportunity, that he would not fall into one of three positions: he would evade with irrelevancies, he would divert the conversation elsewhere, he would display anger, hatred, and displeasure, or he would sit silent, abashed, with shoulders drooping, head bowed, brooding, at a loss for words, just like Sarabha the wanderer."
an3.64:10.4: Then the *Bhagavā*, having roared the lion's roar three times in the wanderers' park on the bank of the Sappinī, departed through the air.
an3.64:11.1: Then those wanderers, soon after the *Bhagavā* had left, assailed Sarabha the wanderer on all sides with stinging words:
an3.64:11.2: "Just as, friend Sarabha, an old jackal in a great forest, thinking 'I will roar the lion's roar,' roars only a jackal's howl, only a bheraṇḍaka's howl;
an3.64:11.3: in just the same way, friend Sarabha, away from the ascetic Gotama, thinking 'I will roar the lion's roar,' you howl only a jackal's howl, only a bheraṇḍaka's howl.
an3.64:11.4: Just as, friend Sarabha, a water-bird, thinking 'I will make the call of a peacock,' makes only a water-bird's call;
an3.64:11.5: in just the same way, friend Sarabha, away from the ascetic Gotama, thinking 'I will make the call of a peacock,' you make only a water-bird's call.
an3.64:11.6: Just as, friend Sarabha, a bull thinks it should bellow deeply in an empty cattle-shed;
an3.64:11.7: in just the same way, friend Sarabha, away from the ascetic Gotama, you think you should bellow deeply."
an3.64:11.8: And so those wanderers assailed Sarabha the wanderer on all sides with stinging words.
an3.64:2.1: Then those *bhikkhus*, having walked for alms in Rājagaha, returned from the alms round after the meal and approached the *Bhagavā*. Having approached and paid homage to the *Bhagavā*, they sat to one side. Seated to one side, those *bhikkhus* said this to the *Bhagavā*:
an3.64:2.2: "*Bhante*, a wanderer named Sarabha has recently left this *dhamma* and *vinaya* [*dhammavinayā*].
an3.64:2.3: He is speaking these words in an assembly at Rājagaha:
an3.64:2.4: 'I have understood the *dhamma* of the ascetics, the sons of the Sakyan.
an3.64:2.5: And having understood the *dhamma* of the ascetics, the sons of the Sakyan, that is why I have left that *dhamma* and *vinaya* [*dhammavinayā*].'
an3.64:2.6: It would be good, *bhante*, if the *Bhagavā* were to go to the wanderers' park on the bank of the Sappinī, to where Sarabha the wanderer is, out of compassion."
an3.64:3.1: Then the *Bhagavā*, in the evening, having emerged from seclusion, went to the wanderers' park on the bank of the Sappinī, to where Sarabha the wanderer was. Having approached, he sat down on the prepared seat. Seated, the *Bhagavā* said this to Sarabha the wanderer:
an3.64:3.2: "Is it true, Sarabha, that you say this:
an3.64:3.3: 'I have understood the *dhamma* of the ascetics, the sons of the Sakyan.
an3.64:3.4: And having understood the *dhamma* of the ascetics, the sons of the Sakyan, that is why I have left that *dhamma* and *vinaya* [*dhammavinayā*]'?"
an3.64:3.5: When this was said, Sarabha the wanderer was silent.
an3.64:4.1: A second time the *Bhagavā* said this to Sarabha the wanderer:
an3.64:4.2: "Speak, Sarabha — what have you understood of the *dhamma* of the ascetics, the sons of the Sakyan?
an3.64:4.3: If it is incomplete for you, I will complete it.
an3.64:4.4: But if it is complete for you, I will rejoice in it."
an3.64:4.5: A second time, Sarabha the wanderer was silent.
an3.64:5.1: A third time the *Bhagavā* said this to Sarabha the wanderer:
an3.64:5.2: "Whatever is known as the *dhamma* of the ascetics, the sons of the Sakyan — speak, Sarabha: what have you understood of the *dhamma* of the ascetics, the sons of the Sakyan?
an3.64:5.3: If it is incomplete for you, I will complete it.
an3.64:5.4: But if it is complete for you, I will rejoice in it."
an3.64:5.5: A third time, Sarabha the wanderer was silent.
an3.64:6.1: Then those wanderers said this to Sarabha the wanderer:
an3.64:6.2: "Whatever you might ask of the ascetic Gotama, friend Sarabha, the ascetic Gotama offers you the same.
an3.64:6.3: Speak, friend Sarabha — what have you understood of the *dhamma* of the ascetics, the sons of the Sakyan?
an3.64:6.4: If it is incomplete for you, the ascetic Gotama will complete it.
an3.64:6.5: But if it is complete for you, the ascetic Gotama will rejoice in it."
an3.64:6.6: When this was said, Sarabha the wanderer sat silent, abashed, with shoulders drooping, head bowed, brooding, at a loss for words.
an3.64:7.1: Then the *Bhagavā*, knowing that Sarabha the wanderer was sitting silent, abashed, with shoulders drooping, head bowed, brooding, at a loss for words, said this to those wanderers:
an3.64:8.1: "Whoever, wanderers, were to say this to me:
an3.64:8.2: 'You claim to be a fully self-awakened one, yet these *dhammas* have not been fully awakened to by you' — that person I would thoroughly question, probe, and examine on this matter.
an3.64:8.3: That person, when thoroughly questioned, probed, and examined by me — it is impossible, there is no opportunity, that he would not fall into one of three positions: he would evade with irrelevancies, he would divert the conversation elsewhere, he would display anger, hatred, and displeasure, or he would sit silent, abashed, with shoulders drooping, head bowed, brooding, at a loss for words, just like Sarabha the wanderer.
an3.64:9.1: Whoever, wanderers, were to say this to me:
an3.64:9.2: 'You claim to be one whose *āsavas* are destroyed, yet these *āsavas* have not been fully destroyed by you' — that person I would thoroughly question, probe, and examine on this matter.
an3.64:9.3: That person, when thoroughly questioned, probed, and examined by me — it is impossible, there is no opportunity, that he would not fall into one of three positions: he would evade with irrelevancies, he would divert the conversation elsewhere, he would display anger, hatred, and displeasure, or he would sit silent, abashed, with shoulders drooping, head bowed, brooding, at a loss for words, just like Sarabha the wanderer.
